description:
# Why

bl-e454 made `populate_on_close` resolve `delivered_in` via the cross-repo
cache when `resolve_remote` is set and the local tag scan misses. But the
bl-7523 auto-tag that fires whenever `delivered_in` is set is unconditional
(src/delivery.rs):

    task.delivered_in = Some(sha);
    task.delivered_repo = Some(crate::repo_url::current(repo_root));

`repo_url::current` is the *closing clone's* origin. That is correct when
the close runs in the clone that produced the squash — the bl-7523
assumption. It is wrong in exactly the scenario `--resolve-remote` exists
for: a hub or sibling clone closing a task whose code landed in another repo.

Concretely, on a bare task hub closing a task delivered to code repo B:

- before close: `delivered_repo = <repo B>` (set by review, or by a bridge
  sync hook)
- after `bl close --resolve-remote`: `delivered_in` is populated (good) but
  `delivered_repo` is overwritten to `<hub URL>` (wrong)

A later `bl show --resolve-remote` then chases the hub URL, which carries no
code integration branch — the tag scan misses and provenance is lost. The
flag that was supposed to *recover* provenance instead destroys it. A reader
is worse off with the flag than without it.

# Fix

`resolve_with` already returns `Delivery { resolved_repo }` — the URL the
sha was actually resolved through (the task's `delivered_repo` on a remote
hit, `repo_url::current` on a local hit). `populate_on_close` calls
`resolve_with` today but consumes only `.sha`. Thread `.resolved_repo`
through: when the sha came from the resolution, set `task.delivered_repo`
to `resolved_repo` instead of recomputing `repo_url::current`.

- Local hit: `resolved_repo` already equals `repo_url::current` — byte-identical.
- Remote hit: `resolved_repo` is the source URL — the correct provenance.
- `manual_repo` (`--delivered-repo`) still overrides last, unchanged.
- `manual_sha` (`--delivered`) path: no resolution ran, so keep the
  `repo_url::current` auto-tag — the operator handed a sha they have
  locally, so bl-7523's assumption still holds there.

# Tests

- close on a clone with no local history, `delivered_repo` pointing at a
  fetchable URL with the `[bl-xxxx]` commit, `resolve_remote = true`: assert
  `delivered_repo` ends up as the *source* URL, not the closing clone's.
- regression: a local-hit close still auto-tags `repo_url::current`.
- The bl-e454 test `populate_on_close_remote_on_resolves_via_delivered_repo`
  currently asserts the *wrong* behavior on purpose and documents it as such
  — that assertion flips when this lands.

# Origin

bl-e454 review pass — flagged in the handoff notes. Noticed while writing
the bl-e454 tests and wrongly rationalized as 'consistent with bl-7523';
it is not, because bl-7523's auto-tag assumes a same-clone close.
title: populate_on_close: remote-resolved sha clobbers delivered_repo with the wrong URL
